name: Dablot Prejjesne
author: Abstract Games factory
description: The Sami battle game from Frostviken, Lapland (recorded by Nils Keyland, 1921): a Sami king, prince and 28 warriors against a landlord, his son and 28 tenant farmers on a 72-point Alquerque-style lattice. All pieces step one point along a drawn line and capture by short jump with chains, but only an enemy of equal or lower rank may be taken. Capture everything or trap the opponent to win; two lone kings are a draw, and two lone equal-rank pieces must fight to the death in single combat.
